title: Aureline chronology replay support class packet
description: Boundary schema for one chronology_replay_support_record produced by the qualify-chronology-capture-and-replay-support-classes truth packet. The record declares, per debug lane, the replay support class (supported / limited / view_only / unsupported / policy_blocked), chronology capture state (recorded / not_recorded / restart_with_recording_available / capture_unsupported), mapping-quality badge (exact / approximate / partial / unavailable / stale / mismatched), replay scope (local / remote / notebook_bridge), inspector-state vocabulary (live / snapshot / stale / limited / unavailable), and restart-with-recording posture — so debugger UI, support exports, and release reviewers always read one attributable source instead of deriving capability from adjacent rows. Raw paths, raw command lines, raw payload bytes, raw secret material, and raw prose never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and short reviewable labels. Adding a new class to any closed vocabulary is additive-minor and bumps schema_version; repurposing an existing token is breaking.

schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the chronology replay support class packet.

opaque_ref.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe inside debugger UI state, support bundles, and release-evidence rows. Raw absolute paths, raw URLs, raw signing material, raw secret values, and raw payload bytes never appear here.

short_label.description: Short redaction-safe label rendered verbatim by debugger UI, support-bundle preview, and release reviewers.

lane_class.description: Closed lane vocabulary. local_lane covers local-host debug sessions; remote_helper_lane covers SSH and remote-agent sessions; container_lane covers container-attached sessions; notebook_bridge_lane covers notebook-kernel debugger bridge sessions.

row_class.description: Closed row-class vocabulary. chronology_quality is the lane headline. support_class_admission admits one replay_support_class. capture_state_admission admits one chronology_capture_state_class. mapping_quality_badge_admission admits one mapping_quality_badge_class. replay_scope_admission admits one replay_scope_class. inspector_state_admission admits one inspector_state_class. replay_surface_binding attests a debug surface. restart_posture_admission admits one restart_with_recording_class. lineage_admission binds the stable execution_context_id. known_limit and downgrade_automation carry disclosed gaps.

support_class.description: Row promotion grade. launch_stable is the M4 stable grade. launch_stable_below, beta_grade_only, preview_only, and unsupported are the precise narrowed labels; each narrowed row MUST carry a non-null disclosure_ref. support_unbound never qualifies for stable promotion.

replay_support_class.description: Closed replay support class vocabulary. supported: the lane exposes a full reverse-step and reverse-continue set backed by a recorded session. limited: the lane supports a subset (e.g. snapshot-only, no reverse_continue). view_only: the lane can inspect imported or exported recordings but cannot step backward. unsupported: the runtime or toolchain cannot host any form of reverse-step or replay. policy_blocked: the capability exists but is disabled by policy.

chronology_capture_state_class.description: Closed chronology capture state vocabulary. recorded: a valid recording is attached and replay is possible. not_recorded: the session is live with no recording engaged. restart_with_recording_available: the session can be restarted with recording enabled. capture_unsupported: the current backend or runtime cannot produce a chronology recording.

mapping_quality_badge_class.description: Closed mapping-quality badge vocabulary. exact: source / symbol mapping is exact and verified against build ID. approximate: mapping is inferred but not verified field-for-field. partial: only a subset of frames or symbols are mapped. unavailable: symbol or source-map data is absent. stale: mapping data is older than the current build. mismatched: the build ID on the recording does not match the loaded artifact.

replay_scope_class.description: Closed replay scope vocabulary. local_scope: recording and replay run on the local host. remote_scope: recording or replay runs through a remote agent. notebook_bridge_scope: replay is available only via a notebook-kernel bridge, which may impose additional limitations.

inspector_state_class.description: Closed inspector state vocabulary for variable, stack, and evaluate surfaces during replay. live: value reflects the current replay frame. snapshot: value reflects a captured snapshot from the recording. stale: value is older than the current replay position. limited: value is partially available. unavailable: value cannot be retrieved from the recording.

restart_with_recording_class.description: Closed restart-with-recording posture vocabulary. available: the session supports restart-with-recording and the action is enabled. unavailable_unsupported_backend: the current backend does not support recording. unavailable_policy_blocked: recording is policy-disabled. unavailable_no_live_session: there is no live session to restart.

replay_surface_class.description: Closed replay surface vocabulary. Each surface must bind typed replay_support_class, inspector_state_class, and mapping_quality_badge_class instead of falling back to generic error copy or silently hiding controls.

evidence_class.description: Closed evidence class vocabulary. evidence_unbound never qualifies a launch_stable row.

known_limit_class.description: Closed known-limit vocabulary. none_declared means no gap is registered. All other values must pair with a disclosure_ref.

downgrade_automation_class.description: Closed downgrade-automation vocabulary. none means no automation is bound.

confidence_class.description: Closed confidence vocabulary. confidence_unbound never qualifies a launch_stable row.

attests_inspector_state_preserved.description: True when the row's replay surface preserves the closed inspector_state_class vocabulary (required for variable_inspector_surface, call_stack_surface, and evaluate_surface).

attests_mapping_quality_preserved.description: True when the row's replay surface preserves the closed mapping_quality_badge_class vocabulary (required for call_stack_surface, variable_inspector_surface, evaluate_surface, support_export_surface, and compare_card_surface).

attests_replay_read_only.description: True when the row attests replay is read-only and no live mutation path is active. Must be true for any row whose replay_support_class is supported, limited, or view_only.

raw_source_material_excluded.description: True when raw debugger payloads, raw stack frames, raw memory bytes, raw recording chunks, raw command lines, and raw env bytes are excluded from this row.

secrets_excluded.description: True when secrets, tokens, and signing material are excluded from this row.

ambient_authority_excluded.description: True when ambient host authority is excluded from this row.

chronology_replay_support_row.description: One row in the chronology replay support class truth packet. Each row carries the full closed vocabulary so downstream consumers never derive support class from adjacent rows.

consumer_projection.description: One consumer projection that must preserve all closed vocabularies verbatim. Surfaces MUST NOT mint local copies, flatten support-class into generic error copy, or silently hide replay controls.

chronology_replay_support_record.description: The chronology replay support class truth packet. One packet covers the four debug lanes (local, remote_helper, container, notebook_bridge) and certifies that every surface reading replay support, capture state, mapping quality, inspector state, and restart-with-recording posture does so from this packet rather than from adjacent capability rows or improvised UI copy.
